learning_outcomes.description: Communicate goals and expectations in a cross-cultural environment to negotiate project outcomes.
Develop IT systems that deliver solutions that are suitable for a global audience.
Work in a cross-cultural team to deliver an IT system.

assessments.due_date:
6/07/2026 - 10/07/2026

The demonstration will be conducted in-person during class time. Your entire team should be at the presentation. Only one or two people are required to make the presentation, but any team member may be asked questions related to the project.
assessments.mode: Activity/ Performance, Oral, Product/ Artefact/ Multimedia
assessments.category: Computer Code, Practical/ Demonstration, Presentation, Product/ Design, Project
assessments.task_description:
Teams will present the current state of their prototype at the end of the first development sprint. The presentation will include a demonstration of the prototype's current functionality. The presentation should highlight the value delivered at this stage and what you plan to deliver by the end of the next sprint. All project artefacts that make up the prototype need to be committed and pushed to your repository. The commit of the work demonstrated is to be tagged as "sprint1" in your repository.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst you may use AI and MT technologies in developing your prototype, successful completion of assessment in this course will require you to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. You must include in your repository a file AI.md that indicates the AI and MT tools that you used and the extent of their use. (e.g. All code was written by providing copilot with class descriptions and then revising the generated code. Classes A and B were produced by the following prompts to ChatGPT and were then adapted to work in the assignment's context.)

To pass this assessment you will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of your submitted project artefacts, independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission of prototype artefacts is through the project source repository created for your team. You must give the course coordinator access to your repository.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
10/07/2026 4:00 pm

Submission deadline is 16:00 China Standard Time.
assessments.task_description:
You will conduct a retrospective with your project team. You are to identify issues in your current development process and practices. You should determine which of the processes or practices need to be changed during the next sprint in order to successfully deliver your intended prototype. You need to decide on concrete, practical actions you will do during the next sprint to deliver the improvements.

You should not be overly ambitious in what you decide to change in your process or practices. You are expected to identify more issues than you can resolve in the next sprint. You should focus on those changes that will provide the greatest improvement to how your team works together.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst you may use AI and Machine Translation (MT) technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require you to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI will provide only limited support and guidance. You may find it helpful to use AI tools to help plan and structure a retrospective session. You may not use AI tools to generate the text of your retrospective. That would constitute academic misconduct, as you would not be reflecting on your team's progress and considering real issues in your team. You could use AI tools to suggest approaches that could be used to improve issues with how your team is working together.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment you will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of the issues within your team and strategies for improving some of these issues, independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission via the link provided on BlackBoard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
17/07/2026

The presentation will take place in a formal scheduled presentation session at DNUI.
assessments.mode: Activity/ Performance, Oral, Product/ Artefact/ Multimedia
assessments.category: Computer Code, Presentation, Product/ Design, Project
assessments.task_description:
Teams will give a formal presentation of the business case for their enterprise. The business case is to be presented as a pitch to potential angel investors. The presentation should include a demonstration of your product prototype, with an emphasis on convincing investors of its value. You do not have time to provide an extended demonstration of your prototype or a detailed explanation of all its features. You need to make the demonstration a story that demonstrates the key value delivered by the product and why your target audience would want to use it.

In accordance with UQ assessment policy, your presentation will be recorded.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst you may use AI and MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require you to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. You must include in your repository a file AI.md that indicates the AI and MT tools that you used and the extent of their use. (e.g. All code was written by providing copilot with class descriptions and then revising the generated code. Classes A and B were produced by the following prompts to ChatGPT and were then adapted to work in the assignment's context.) You must also include in your repository files called AI-log-name.md that contains a log of the history of your interactions with the AI tool(s) that you used while developing your solution. Each student must provide their own AI-log-name.md file, where name is your full name, as recorded in UQ student systems.

To pass this assessment you will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of your project and its business case, independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The presentation will be made to an assessment panel and other students. The presentation may be lived streamed.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: The learning outcomes for this course are developed and demonstrated through your team's project work. Your effectiveness as a team member will have a direct impact on what you learn and achieve in this course. Your individual contribution to the teamwork and processes will be assessed to determine if you have achieved the course outcomes of communicating and working effectively in a cross-cultural environment to deliver an innovative IT system.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You will be assessed by observation of the teaching team.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Project Initiation

Start-up China Program

This is an intensive program to develop your ability to work in a multi-cultural team to deliver an innovative software product. Program activities and their times are indicated in the provided program schedule.

Week 1 (22 - 26 June) will take place on-line. During this week you will get to know your project team and make initial plans for the project that you will develop in China.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

Program activities and their times are indicated in the provided program schedule. Week 1 (22 - 26 June) will take place on-line. During this week you will get to know your project team and make initial plans for the project that you will develop in China.

learning_activities.topic:
Project Development

Start-up China Program

This is an intensive program to develop your ability to work in a multi-cultural team to deliver an innovative software product. Program activities and their times are indicated in the provided program schedule.

Weeks 2 to 4 of the program (29 June to 17 July) will take place in-person at the DNUI campus in Dalian. Over weeks 2 to 4 you will work on developing your project, supported by the content delivered during the program. You will also put together a business case for your enterprise.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Project Development Start-up China Program This is an intensive program to develop your ability to work in a multi-cultural team to deliver an innovative software product. Program activities and their times are indicated in the provided program schedule. Weeks 2 to 4 of the program (29 June to 17 July) will take place in-person at the DNUI campus in Dalian. Over weeks 2 to 4 you will work on developing your project, supported by the content delivered during the program. You will also put together a business case for your enterprise.

learning_activities.topic:
Cultural Activities

Start-up China Program

This is an intensive program to develop your ability to work in a multi-cultural team to deliver an innovative software product. Program activities and their times are indicated in the provided program schedule.

Weeks 2 to 5 of the program (29 June to 21 July) will take place in China. There will be a variety of cultural and language learning activities scheduled each week. You are required to participate in all the cultural and language learning activities, aside from the optional excursion to Shanghai. These activities are designed to help you learn how to work effectively in a cross cultural team.

Learning outcomes: L03
